q: When solutions of an acid and a base are mixed, an ___ occurs.
Salts are ___ that completely dissociate into ions in water.
The term ___ describes the reaction of an anion or a cation of a salt, or both, with water.
Salt hydrolysis usually affects the ___ of a solution.
Solutions of salts of strong bases and strong acids are ___ ($pH = 7$).
The cation $Na^{+}$ and the anion $Cl^{-}$ of $NaCl$ do not ___.
Sodium ethanoate ($CH_{3}COONa$) is the salt of the ___ and the weak acid ethanoic acid.
The anion $CH_{3}COO^{-}$ is a ___ conjugate base of the weak acid $CH_{3}COOH$.
The solution of sodium ethanoate becomes ___ ($pH > 7$).
Ammonium chloride ($NH_{4}Cl$) is the salt of the ___ and the strong acid hydrochloric acid.
The cation $NH_{4}^{+}$ of $NH_{4}Cl$ is a ___ conjugate acid of the weak base $NH_{4}OH$.
The solution of ammonium chloride becomes ___ ($pH < 7$).
For a salt derived from a weak acid and a weak base, both the ___ hydrolyse.
If $K_{b}$ for the anion is greater than $K_{a}$ for the cation, the solution must be ___.
If $K_{b}$ for the anion is smaller than $K_{a}$ for the cation, the solution will be ___.
